introSection: Cobra's Curse is a steel spinning roller coaster built by German firm Mack Rides located at Busch Gardens Tampa in Tampa, Florida, USA. It has an elevator lift.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In order to build the ride, the King Tut's Tomb attraction was closed and the building that contained it was expanded. Parts of Tut's Tomb were integrated into the queue line for Cobra's Curse.

Construction on Cobra's Curse began in January 2015.

The ride was announced on May 28, 2015.

The track pieces arrived on site in October 2015, and construction began shortly after.

In November 2015, a 1:120 scale model of the ride built by Skyline Attractions was unveiled at Mack Rides' booth at the 2025 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

The 30,000 pound snake head prop and the top of the vertical lift were installed in February 2016.

Cobra's Curse had its first test run in April 2016, and opened on June 17, 2016.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
The ride is themed around an archeological expedition into an abandoned Egyptian tomb. The queue contains live snakes and projection mapping. The ride includes an statue of a snake.

Cobra's Curse has a forward-facing section, a backward-facing section and a free-spinning section.

fullText:
Cobra's Curse is a steel spinning roller coaster built by German firm Mack Rides located at Busch Gardens Tampa in Tampa, Florida, USA. It has an elevator lift.

In order to build the ride, the King Tut's Tomb attraction was closed and the building that contained it was expanded. Parts of Tut's Tomb were integrated into the queue line for Cobra's Curse.

Construction on Cobra's Curse began in January 2015.

The ride was announced on May 28, 2015.

The track pieces arrived on site in October 2015, and construction began shortly after.

In November 2015, a 1:120 scale model of the ride built by Skyline Attractions was unveiled at Mack Rides' booth at the 2025 IAAPA Expo in Orlando, Florida, USA.

The 30,000 pound snake head prop and the top of the vertical lift were installed in February 2016.

Cobra's Curse had its first test run in April 2016, and opened on June 17, 2016.

The ride is themed around an archeological expedition into an abandoned Egyptian tomb. The queue contains live snakes and projection mapping. The ride includes an statue of a snake.

Cobra's Curse has a forward-facing section, a backward-facing section and a free-spinning section.
